next_steps: Blood pressure management to treat hypertension
Lumbar puncture to rule out infectious meningoencephalitis
Electroencephalogram (EEG) to evaluate for subclinical seizure activity
Review of chronic medication and blood transfusion history for potential triggers
Consultation with neurology and hematology subspecialists

differential_diagnoses: Symptomatic epilepsy secondary to chronic sickle cell-related brain injury
Drug-induced neurotoxicity (e.g., opioid-related seizure threshold reduction)
Metabolic encephalopathy secondary to acute sickle cell crisis
next_steps: Assess coagulation profile including anti-Xa levels to guide safe invasive procedures
Perform lumbar puncture to rule out infection, if coagulation status allows
Comprehensive toxicological screening for atypical substances or interactions
Multidisciplinary review involving hematology, neurology, and neurocritical care teams
Close monitoring of renal function and electrolyte balance
